name: Movement Quality Challenge
why: Gamifies fundamental movement patterns while building body awareness.

application.headline: A circuit of 6–8 fundamental movement stations with athlete self-assessment.
notes: Athletes perform self-assessment at each station using a simple 1–3 rating.
Coach provides specific upgrade cues based on individual needs.
Second round focuses on applying upgrades with partner feedback.

science: Self-assessment improves proprioception and movement awareness while the progressive nature builds motor control before introducing external load. Each of these warm-ups should take 8–12 minutes and can be customised based on the day's training focus, available equipment, and athlete development level. The key is creating a systematic approach that prepares both body and mind for optimal training performance.
